info.name: BahirdarRide API — Complete v2
info.description:
Complete API collection covering Auth, Driver Onboarding, Location Tracking, Matching Engine, Trip Lifecycle, Vehicle Categories, and Admin.

## Quick Start
1. Set baseUrl = http://localhost:3000/api/v1
2. OTP bypass in dev: use 1234
3. Tokens auto-saved — run Login first in each section
4. Run folders top-to-bottom for correct flow

## Auto-captured variables
- riderToken, driverToken, adminToken
- tripId (from POST /trips)
- driverId, categoryId

## Matching Engine Flow
POST /trips → status:searching → matching worker picks job → driver gets trip:offer socket → driver accepts → rider gets trip:matched socket

## Socket Events (not testable via Postman — use wscat or mobile app)
  trip:offer, trip:matched, trip:no_drivers
  trip:driver_arrived, trip:started, trip:completed

Complete API collection covering Auth, Driver Onboarding, Location Tracking, Matching Engine, Trip Lifecycle, Vehicle Categories, and Admin.

## Security Headers
- X-Idempotency-Key: <uuid> — on POST /trips to prevent duplicate requests

## Rate Limits
- POST /trips: 3 per rider per 10 minutes → 429
- POST /drivers/me/location: 1 per 2 seconds → 429

## Security Error Codes
- TOKEN_EXPIRED: 401 — token expired or session revoked after logout
- CANNOT_CANCEL_IN_PROGRESS: 409 — rider cannot cancel an active trip
- PAYMENT_ALREADY_EXISTS: 409 — trip already has a payment record
- OUTSIDE_SERVICE_AREA: 422 — pickup outside Bahir Dar boundary
- ACTIVE_TRIP_EXISTS: 409 — rider already has an active trip
- DRIVER_ALREADY_ON_TRIP: 409 — driver has an active trip
- GPS spoofing: >200 km/h jump → flag; 3 flags → driver suspended silently
- Decline cooldown: 5 consecutive declines → 10-min cooldown key in Redis

item.name: 🏥 1. Health & Redis
item.description: Server health and Redis connection tests. Run these first to confirm infrastructure is up.

request.description: Returns server status, DB connection, Redis connection.
Confirms Redis is connected and responding. Expect: PONG
Lists all driver IDs currently in Redis GEO set (driver:online). These are drivers sending location updates.
Finds all online drivers within radius using Redis GEOSEARCH.
Get full Redis hash data for a specific driver. Replace driverId variable.
Writes a test key then reads it back. Confirms Redis read/write is working.

item.name: 🚴 2. Auth — Rider
item.description: Rider registration and login flow. OTP bypass: use 1234 in development.

request.description: Check if phone is registered before sending OTP. Returns: { exists, role, account_status, can_login }
Send OTP to rider phone. In dev mode: use 1234 to bypass.
Register new rider. Returns JWT access + refresh tokens. Token auto-saved to riderToken.
Login existing rider. Token auto-saved to riderToken.
Get new access token using refresh token. Refresh tokens last 30 days.

item.name: 🚗 3. Auth — Driver Onboarding Wizard
item.description: 7-step driver registration flow. Run steps in order. Driver starts with phone+name only, then completes profile.

request.description: Check if driver phone is already registered.
Send OTP to driver phone. In dev mode: use 1234 to bypass.
Creates user + empty driver_profiles row. Returns token. Full profile collected in next steps.
Submit driver license and national ID details.
Submit vehicle details. category must be one of the DB enum values: economy | comfort | business
Upload front of driver license. Field name must be 'file'.

formdata.description: Any field name works: file, avatar, image, photo. Max 5MB.

request.description:
Upload driver profile photo.
- Endpoint: POST /drivers/me/avatar (also works: /drivers/me/profile-image)
- Field name: any (file, avatar, image, photo — all accepted)
- Max size: 5MB
- Stored in Supabase Storage under driver_profiles/{phone}-{name}/
Returns full driver profile including account_status (pending|approved|rejected).

item.name: 📍 4. Driver — Location Tracking (Redis)
item.description: Driver sends location to Redis every 3 seconds. Redis stores in GEOADD set + Hash with 30s TTL. If driver stops sending, they auto-expire from Redis.

request.description: Driver app calls this every 3 seconds. Stores in Redis GEOADD (driver:online) and Hash (driver:loc:{userId}) with 30s TTL. Also fire-and-forget updates PostgreSQL current_location.
Returns driver's own current location from Redis. Returns null if TTL expired (offline).
Removes driver from Redis GEO set and deletes Hash key. Driver disappears from nearby search immediately.

item.name: 🗺️ 5. Geo & Fare Estimation
item.description: Route calculation via OSRM + fare estimation per vehicle category. Call fare-estimate BEFORE booking to show the rider price options for each vehicle type.

request.description: Returns real driving distance_km, duration_min and polyline from OSRM. Falls back to Haversine if OSRM is not running.

⭐ MAIN ENDPOINT for rider price screen.

Returns:
- distance_km + duration_min (from OSRM)
- surge_multiplier (from geo zones)
- estimates[] — one entry per active vehicle category with:
  - vehicle_category: 'economy' | 'comfort' | 'business'
  - estimated_fare_etb (final price to show rider)
  - cancellation_fee_etb
  - surge_multiplier

Formula: (base_fare + distance_km × per_km + duration_min × per_min) × surge, min min_fare

Pass vehicle_category from the estimate the rider selects into POST /trips.

exec: console.log('Fare estimates loaded. Top category:', r.data.estimates[0].vehicle_category, '=', r.data.estimates[0].estimated_fare_etb, 'ETB');

request.description: Returns surge_multiplier for a pickup point. 1.0 = normal, >1.0 = surge pricing active.
Converts coordinates to a human-readable address. Use when rider pins a location on the map.
Shows nearby available drivers on the map before rider books. Uses PostGIS ST_DWithin.

item.name: 🧠 6. Matching Engine — Full Flow Test
item.description:
Tests the complete matching flow end-to-end.

PREREQUISITES:
1. Driver must be online (sent location to Redis via POST /drivers/me/location)
2. Driver account_status must be 'active' (approved by admin)
3. Driver vehicle category must match rider's selected category
4. Rider must be logged in (riderToken set)

FLOW:
1. POST /trips → status: searching → startMatching() fires
2. Redis GEORADIUS finds drivers within 5km (expands to 15km if none)
3. Drivers ranked by score: proximity 50% + rating 30% + acceptance 20%
4. Driver gets socket event: trip:offer (room: driver:{userId})
5. Driver calls POST /drivers/trips/accept
6. Trip status → matched, rider gets socket event: trip:status
7. Trip progresses: driver_arrived → in_progress → completed

NOTE: Socket room is driver:{users.id} NOT driver:{driver_profiles.id}

request.description: Creates trip in DB (status: searching) and pushes job to Redis queue:matching. Matching worker picks it up immediately. tripId auto-saved.

item.name: ▶ STEP 2 — Check Trip Status (Poll while searching)

request.description:
Poll this every 5 seconds as fallback if socket event is missed.
Status flow: searching → matched → driver_arrived → in_progress → completed
Driver accepts the trip offer. Updates trip status to matched, assigns driver, marks driver busy in Redis.

item.name: ▶ STEP 3b — Driver Accepts Offer (legacy body param)

request.description: Same as STEP 3 but uses legacy POST body format. Kept for backwards compatibility.

Driver explicitly declines the offer (or auto-triggered after 15s timeout by the matching worker). Sets trip_driver_offers.status = 'declined'. Matching worker's DB poll detects this immediately and moves to the next ranked driver (e.g. Kebede). No body required.
Get driver's live location from Redis. Call every 3s to animate driver pin on map. Returns: { is_online, lat, lng, heading, speed_kmh }

Driver taps 'I Arrived'. Updates trips.status = 'driver_arrived'. Emits trip:driver_arrived to rider's socket room (user:{riderId}). Sends FCM push to rider: 'Your driver has arrived!'. Response includes trip + rider { id, name, phone } for driver's Waiting screen.

Driver taps START TRIP (rider in car). Updates trips SET status='in_progress', started_at=NOW(). Emits trip:started to trip room (both rider + driver) with: { trip_id, started_at, dropoff_address, distance_km, estimated_fare_etb }. HTTP response includes same fields for clients that miss the socket event.

Driver taps COMPLETE TRIP. Does 6 things atomically: (1) OSRM actual road distance pickup→dropoff, (2) recalculate final fare (base+distance+time), (3) UPDATE trips status='completed' + final fare + actual_distance_km + actual_duration_min, (4) INSERT payments (status=pending), (5) UPSERT driver_earnings daily row (gross/platform_cut/net), (6) UPDATE driver_profiles status='online' + Redis driver:meta={status:online}. Emits trip:completed to rider: { finalFare, distance, fare_breakdown }. Driver receives trip:complete_confirmed with driver_payout_etb.
Rider submits rating after trip completed. Updates driver avg rating in driver_profiles.
Driver declines the offer. Updates trip_driver_offers status to declined. Matching worker detects this and tries the next ranked driver automatically.
Rider cancels trip. Status becomes cancelled_by_rider. Cancellation fee may apply after driver is matched.
Shows driver dots on map before rider books. Returns drivers from Redis within radius.
Returns driver's current active trip (status: matched, driver_arrived, or in_progress). Returns null if no active trip.
Returns rider's full trip history ordered by created_at DESC.

item.name: 🏷️ 6. Vehicle Categories — Public
item.description: Public endpoints used by rider and driver apps. No authentication required.

request.description:
Returns only active (is_active=true) categories sorted by display_order. Used by:
- Rider app: 'Choose ride type' screen
- Driver app: 'Select your vehicle category' during registration

No auth required.

item.name: 👑 7. Admin — Login
item.description: Admin login uses email + password (not OTP). Super admin is seeded via: npm run seed:super-admin

request.description: Admin login with email + password. Returns JWT with adminRole claim. Token auto-saved to adminToken.

item.name: 👑 8. Admin — Vehicle Categories
item.description:
Manage ride categories. Write operations require super_admin role. Read allowed for all admin roles.

Admin sets up categories FIRST before drivers can register or riders can book.

request.description: Returns all categories including inactive ones. Includes full pricing and metadata.
Full details for one category.

body.raw:
{
  "name": "Bajaj",
  "name_am": "ባጃጅ",
  "description": "Three-wheel bajaj for short trips",
  "description_am": "ለአጭር ጉዞ ሶስት bága bajaj",
  "icon": "tuk-tuk",
  "capacity": 3,
  "base_fare": 12.00,
  "per_km_rate": 4.00,
  "per_minute_rate": 0.25,
  "minimum_fare": 20.00,
  "is_active": true,
  "display_order": 5
}

request.description: Create new vehicle category. name must be unique. categoryId auto-saved.
Partial update — only send fields you want to change.
Flips is_active automatically. No body needed. Active → Inactive → Active. Inactive categories are hidden from rider + driver apps.
Set display order for all categories at once. Send ALL items with their new display_order values.
Deletes category permanently. Returns 409 CONFLICT if active drivers are registered under this category. Use Toggle to disable instead.

item.name: 👑 9. Admin — Driver Management
item.description: Review, approve, reject, and manage drivers. Approval unlocks driver to go online and receive trips.

request.description: Returns drivers waiting for approval with their submitted documents.
Full driver profile: personal info, vehicle, license, documents, stats.
Returns all uploaded documents for a driver with file URLs and review status.
Sets account_status = approved. Driver can now go online and receive trips.
Sets account_status = rejected. Reason is stored and can be shown to driver in app.
Suspends an approved driver. Driver cannot go online or receive trips while suspended.
Review individual documents. status: approved | rejected. Admin can approve documents one by one.
Permanently deletes driver and all associated data. Only super_admin can do this.

item.name: 👑 10. Admin — User Management
item.description: Manage all platform users (riders, drivers, admins).

request.description: Create admin panel user. adminRole: admin | finance | operations | support | super_admin

item.name: 👑 11. Admin — Trips
item.description: View and manage all trips on the platform.

request.description: Full trip details including rider info, driver info, vehicle, all offer attempts.
Admin force-cancels a stuck trip. Sets status to cancelled_by_system.
Returns: total trips today, active drivers, total riders, revenue today, pending drivers.
All system config key-value pairs (surge multiplier, max offer attempts, etc.)
Update any system config value. Replace 'surge_multiplier' with the key name.
Admin action audit trail: who did what and when.
Revenue totals, platform commission, refunds.
Process refund for a specific payment.

item.name: 👑 14. Admin — Broadcasts
item.description: Send push notifications to all riders, all drivers, or specific users.

request.description: target: all | riders | drivers
Register FCM token for push notifications. platform: android | ios
